page: Amazon Web Services r8id.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz, with a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For high-speed local storage, the instance includes 11400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory workloads. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scoring, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also rank in the top 10%, facilitating rapid data access. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 245.08 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring in the top tier. Additionally, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions support strong LLM inference speeds, processing 335.69 tokens per second for medium 7B models.

While the instance delivers top-tier sequential memory bandwidth and processing capabilities, its PassMark memory score sits in the average tier, indicating potential latency tradeoffs under specific synthetic memory access patterns. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of massive DDR5 memory capacity with 11.4 TB of local NVMe SSD storage reduces the need for external network-attached storage, optimizing resource density for data-intensive applications. This hardware profile is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, heavy database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 192 vCPUs from 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores clocked at 3.9 GHz, 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 245.08 million operations per second. Although its PassMark memory score is average, its high memory capacity and integrated local NVMe storage offer high resource density, minimizing external storage overhead. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11.4 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated NVMe SSDs for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs via Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
11400 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU and database benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and large-scale data processing
